response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the role specifically demands a C++ developer with 8-12 years building high-performance, multi-threaded, message-broker-based industrial systems. C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (Dec 2014 – Jun 2015) and an academic bachelor thesis; the listed skills section does not even include C++. There is no evidence of IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or message-broker architectures. Primary expertise is in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/GIS platforms — adjacent but not a fit for the core requirement.
strengths: 12+ years of software engineering experience with strong fundamentals and TDD discipline
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput services (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, DLang async I/O)
Background in mentoring, workshops, and technical communication
concerns: Minimal recent C++/STL experience; C++ not even listed in skills section
No demonstrated exposure to IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No experience with industrial automation protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) or message brokers in the required context

key_factors.factor: Strong general software engineering and low-latency systems experience
